meta.title: Farmer Drop — Ledger Posting Simulator Events
meta.purpose: Declarative posting rules interpreted by ledger_simulator.html. Each posting is one balanced transfer {dr, cr, amount_expr} — an event balances by construction; the page self-audits events × branches × parameter sweeps at load and paints the invariant strip red on any imbalance. Spec engine mandate: integer cents, round2 = nearest cent ties-away-from-zero; the demo page uses cent-rounded floats.
meta.basis: Spec layer; reconciles against Volkmar's .7 backend. Fee constants mirror take_rate_params.json. v0.2 incorporates the adversarial-pass fixes (see challenges[]): fee recorded at capture so 1020 mirrors Stripe pending NET; credits respec'd as full-cart tender on a wedge-only rail with the load fee booked; $0.55 retained on ANY refund; refunds capped at the captured total; hopCount canonical 2.
meta.demo_scope: The simulator tracks ONE canonical order per scenario (reset to restart) — a single farmer_paid / dispute_open flag pair cannot represent concurrent orders. Real postings carry effective_at + posted_at per journal row (§6 of the spec page); the demo stamps each entry with its effective day and posted sequence.
meta.amount_expr_language: expr := term (('+'|'-') term)* ; term := factor ('*' factor)* ; factor := NUMBER | param | min(a,b) | max(a,b) | round2(x) | '(' expr ')' | '-' factor. Params are whitelist-checked (typos are validation errors, never silent zeros). No division — deterministic cent math.

notes.takePct: DR-1 OPEN — supported band 7–12%, centered 10%. Parametric everywhere; slider-driven in the simulator.
notes.hopCount: Canonical default 2 (take_rate_params hops.defaultCount). At 2 hops + 10% take the distribution margin is EXACTLY $0 (T1 Flat-Ten closes at 80+10+10=100) — 4020 exists for self-haul/one-hop drops and tiered templates, not as a revenue line to count on. Combinations where farmer 80% + hops + take exceed 100% do not close; the simulator refuses to post them (the same tension take_rate_math shows).
notes.taxRate: Demonstration rate. Real tax is a LINE-ITEM property resolved per pickup jurisdiction (tomatoes grocery-exempt in 37 states; hot sauce varies) — the single rate here stands in for the per-line calculation, and a balanced posting is NOT evidence the tax split is jurisdictionally correct (challenge C-7).

derived.note: distributionMargin is the RESIDUAL (plug) of the goods split — rounding of farmer/hop/take can never unbalance the goods leg; it must be ≥ 0 or the model does not close. creditsGross is the credits-rail cart: goods + $0.25 transfer leg + 0.25% wedge + tax — NO card components (no $0.30, no 2.9%): the credits rail is the Phase-1 fee drop, priced accordingly.

farmer_paid.note: when true, refund/chargeback farmer legs post to the 1200 clawback receivable instead of the 2100 payable

dispute_open.lock_reason: R1: cannot refund outside the dispute process while a dispute is open — double-credit risk.

refunded_fraction.note: cumulative; refunds are CAPPED at the captured total (Stripe: multiple partial refunds up to the total; tax cannot be over-reversed). partial_refund posts min(slider, remaining); full_refund requires 0 refunded and consumes the whole cap; chargeback is disabled once fully refunded.

events.label: Order captured & allocated
events.narrative: Member pays basket + $0.55 + 3.15% + tax by card. Stripe creates the balance_transaction AT CAPTURE with amount/fee/net already split — so the fee is expensed here and 1020 mirrors Stripe pending at NET. The gross is allocated in the same balanced event: farmer, hops, take, margin (plug, ≥0 or the model doesn't close), both surcharges, tax at the ORIGINAL per-line rate. Authorization posts nothing — a two-phase hold, discarded or posted at capture.

postings.memo: platform take (DR-1 parametric; the 3% contractor accrual comes OUT of this — see contractor_accrual)
sales tax @ original rate, stored on line
Stripe fee recorded at capture (balance_transaction amount/fee/net) — pending mirrors NET

events.invariant: net debit to 1020 == derived.gross - derived.stripeFee == balance_transaction.net (the mirror)

events.label: Stripe settles (T+2)
events.narrative: Pending becomes available — a pure money-clock move of the NET (the fee was already recorded at capture). 1010 now mirrors Stripe available.
events.invariant: sum(cr==1020) == derived.gross - derived.stripeFee; 1020 returns to zero

events.label: Farmer payout (Connect transfer)
events.narrative: Farmer payable released to the connected account. Sets farmer_paid — every unwind after this point must claw back via the 1200 receivable instead of debiting an empty payable.

events.label: Contractor commission accrues (monthly)
events.narrative: 3% of the host drop's volume accrues to the recruiting contractor, aged over N months — a time-based accrual, structurally unlike farmer payables (why L6 is its own book). Paid OUT of the platform take: at 10% gross take the platform's NET take is ~7% while a drop is in-window — 4000 and 5200 must be read together.

postings.memo: 3% of drop volume this month, out of the take (window: contractor.windowMonths)

events.invariant: self-balancing accrual pair; net take = 4000 − 5200 while in-window

events.label: Member buys credits (+2% boost)
events.narrative: Cash in by card, $102 of tender out. No sales tax at load — stored value is taxed at redemption. The 2% boost posts against its NAMED funding account (DR-2), and the card fee on the load is booked at capture like any charge: the program costs ~5% of the load (2% boost + ~3.2% card fee) — fund it from wedge/breakage or it is a loss. Credits lots are tracked cash-origin vs boost-origin.

postings.memo: card fee on the load, recorded at capture — the honest cost of the credits program

events.invariant: credits liability rises by basket*(1+boost); platform nets basket − loadFee cash for it

events.label: Member spends credits on a basket
events.narrative: Credits are FULL-CART TENDER (gift-card style: they pay goods, rail fee, and tax). The rail is priced honestly: $0.25 transfer leg + 0.25% wedge — NO card components, because there is no card. This is the Phase-1 fee drop as a posting rule: the member's cart is cheaper by exactly Stripe's 2.9% + $0.30. Tax still assesses at redemption, per line. Requires sufficient credits balance — the tender cannot overdraw the liability.

postings.memo: credits-rail fixed leg $0.25 — transfer only, no card leg
wedge 0.25% only — no 2.9% passthrough on the credits rail

events.invariant: crossfoot: sum(dr==2000) == derived.creditsGross; member saves the card components vs the same basket on Stripe

events.label: Full refund
events.narrative: Member gets the cart back MINUS the $0.55 fixed surcharge — retained on ANY refund, consistently, because it covers Stripe's sunk fixed legs (the fee, already expensed at capture, is never returned by Stripe). Making the member whole on the $0.55 anyway is a policy choice (RQ-7). Revenue reverses through contra 4900; history is never mutated. Tax reverses at the ORIGINAL rate: same period → returned-merchandise credit; period filed → amended return. Requires nothing refunded yet; locked while a dispute is open.

postings.memo: contra-revenue: take + margin + cart surcharge — $0.55 RETAINED
reverse tax @ ORIGINAL rate → Stripe Tax mode=full

events.invariant: crossfoot: sum(cr==1010) == derived.gross - fees.txnSurcharge == Stripe refund.amount. Stripe's entire original fee stays sunk in 5000 (expensed at capture); an optional 5010 disclosure reclass may attribute it to refund losses — net-zero P&L, so it is NOT a posting leg here.

events.label: Partial refund (slider fraction)
events.narrative: THE named pain. The $0.55 is retained (as on any refund — the rule is structural: 4010 is never reversed); everything proportional reverses at the EFFECTIVE fraction = min(slider, remaining cap); tax reverses proportionally AT THE ORIGINAL RATE and must reach Stripe Tax as a mode=partial reversal (flat_amount distributes proportionally across remaining line items) — or, per Stripe's own recommendation for accuracy, as a full-reverse-and-recreate. Cross-period: amended return. Stripe keeps its ENTIRE original fee regardless of the fraction (already sunk at capture). Mixed exempt+taxable baskets make the allocation choice load-bearing — a balanced posting is NOT a jurisdictionally correct one; real refunds tie to lines.

postings.memo: reverse tax pro-rata @ ORIGINAL rate → Stripe Tax mode=partial
farmer already paid → clawback receivable, pro-rata

events.invariant: refund.amount := sum(cr==1010) — the per-leg reversals are AUTHORITATIVE (each leg rounds independently, matching per-line Stripe Tax reversal amounts), ≈ fraction × (gross − $0.55) within ≤2¢. Cumulative refunds are capped at the captured total; tax cannot be over-reversed.

events.label: Clawback recovered from next cycle
events.narrative: The farmer-paid refund left a receivable; it nets in full against the farmer's next payable cycle (or a debit_negative_balances bank pull on the connected account).

postings.memo: net the full receivable against next payable

events.label: Chargeback — funds withdrawn
events.narrative: The network pulls the disputed gross immediately and the $15 fee is expensed on initiation (non-refundable even if won). The pulled amount is a CONTINGENT RECEIVABLE, not a loss — yet. Refunds LOCK while the dispute is open; a fully-refunded charge can no longer be disputed here. Evidence due in 7–21 days; issuer decides in ~60–75.

events.label: Chargeback — won
events.narrative: Funds reinstated; the receivable clears. The $15 stays sunk. Note the clocks: the win is EFFECTIVE at the charge's economic date but KNOWN two months later — valid time ≠ transaction time, which is exactly what the two journal timestamps are for.

events.label: Chargeback — lost
events.narrative: Unwinds like a refund but the tax slice is DIFFERENT: a chargeback is not a refund, so remitted tax is generally unrecoverable via the returned-merchandise credit — it books to Tax Bad-Debt (5310), never back through 2300. The tax authority still gets paid; the platform eats it (bad-debt relief varies, limited in e.g. CA). If the farmer/hop clawback later proves unrecoverable, the write-off branch books to 5300.

postings.memo: contra-revenue (whole revenue leg incl. $0.55 — the receivable writes off at gross)
farmer already paid → clawback receivable (unrecoverable → write off to 5300)

events.invariant: crossfoot: sum(cr==1210) == derived.gross; 1210 returns to zero

challenges.q: credits_spend drained the credits liability by full card-rail GROSS ($111.70), so $102 of credits 'bought' a $111.70 basket — economically incoherent, masked by the crossfoot.
challenges.a: Conceded and respec'd. Credits are full-cart tender (gift-card style — they legitimately pay goods, fees, and tax), but the cart on the credits rail is now priced honestly: goods + $0.25 transfer leg + 0.25% wedge + tax (creditsGross ≈ $108.50 on the canonical basket) with NO card components — the credits rail IS the Phase-1 fee drop, as a posting rule. And the tender can no longer overdraw: credits_spend requires sufficient 2000 balance.

challenges.q: 1020 was defined at GROSS but Stripe records amount/fee/net at capture — pending mirrors NET, so the page's own reconciliation premise (Principle 7) was self-contradicting.
challenges.a: Conceded and fixed. The Stripe fee is now expensed at capture inside the capture event (Dr 5000 / Cr 1020), so 1020 nets to gross − fee = balance_transaction.net, and settle is a pure net move 1020→1010. The mirror invariant now states the balance_transaction identity explicitly.

challenges.q: 'Six ledgers reconciled via double-entry' was never demonstrated — the partition is one book with colored tags; nothing checks per-ledger or inter-ledger balance; L4 with 10 of 26 accounts looks gerrymandered.
challenges.a: Partially conceded. What a partition CAN honestly demonstrate is now shown live: each book's net position is its implied due-to/due-from against the other books, and the six nets sum to zero — the simulator displays per-book nets and checks Σ(book nets) ≡ 0 as a strip invariant. Whether Volkmar's six balance INTERNALLY via explicit due-to/due-from accounts is precisely what we cannot know without the code — RQ-1 now asks that directly. 1210 was re-tagged from L4 to L1 (it is withdrawn cash, not platform-risk bric-a-brac), trimming the grab-bag.

challenges.q: hopCount=1 contradicted the canonical 2-hop model; under the documented default the Distribution Margin (4020) is exactly $0 — a fabricated revenue line; sweeps never varied hops.
challenges.a: Conceded and fixed. Default is now the canonical 2 hops, under which 4020 posts $0.00 — shown, not hidden, with the account re-annotated: it exists for self-haul/one-hop drops and tiered templates, not as a line to count on. A hops control (0–2) was added; infeasible combinations (farmer 80% + hops + take > 100%) refuse to post — the same tension take_rate_math surfaces. Self-audit sweeps now vary hop count.

challenges.q: The credits load's Stripe fee was never booked (a ~5% silent program cost), and the 2.9%-passthrough cart surcharge was still charged on the 'fee-free' credits rail.
challenges.a: Conceded and fixed, both halves. credits_purchase now books the card fee on the load at capture (the program visibly costs ~5.2% of load: 2% boost + ~3.2% fee — fund it or it's a loss, DR-2 adjacent). credits_spend now charges only $0.25 + 0.25% wedge — no passthrough where there is no Stripe cost.

challenges.q: The simulator over-refunded past the captured total (150%+ refunds) with the invariant strip green — balance ≠ correctness, contradicting Stripe's own caps.
challenges.a: Conceded and fixed. Cumulative refunded fraction is tracked; partial_refund posts min(slider, remaining) and disables at the cap; full_refund requires an unrefunded charge; a fully-refunded charge can no longer be disputed. The invariant strip now also says what green means — and what it doesn't.

challenges.q: A single tax rate reversed uniformly across the refund fraction is exactly the mixed-basket misallocation R3 warns about; the green ✓ blesses jurisdictionally wrong reversals.
challenges.a: Partially conceded. A per-line, per-jurisdiction simulation is out of demo scope by design — but the misleading part was the unqualified ✓. The strip and the refund controls now state explicitly: balanced ≠ tax-correct; real refunds tie to lines; refunding an exempt line reverses $0 tax. The spec rule (line-tying, stored original rate) was already the page's position.

challenges.q: Full refund returned the $0.55 while a 100% partial retained it — a $0.55 cliff; and full refund both returned the surcharge AND wrote the whole fee off, double-eating the fixed leg.
challenges.a: Conceded and fixed. The $0.55 is now retained on ANY refund, consistently with its stated rationale (it covers Stripe's sunk fixed legs, which are sunk on refund too). full_refund ≡ partial at 100% (modulo per-leg rounding). Making the member whole on the $0.55 anyway is flagged as a policy choice for Magnus.

challenges.q: The hard-case catalog missed Stripe's multicapture incompatibility (no partial refunds with refund_application_fee/reverse_transfer — manual fee refunds + transfer reversals required), SNAP/EBT tender, and tips/gratuities.
challenges.a: Conceded. All three added (the Connect partial-refund API constraint as HARD — it sits directly on the named-pain workflow; SNAP/EBT and gratuities as ANNOYING). RQ-11 now surfaces the incompatibility explicitly. Catalog is 25 items.

challenges.q: Five 'active' accounts are never posted by any event, and 5300's stated purpose (goods loss on lost disputes) contradicted chargeback_lost, which routes through 4900.
challenges.a: Conceded in part. 5300's purpose was wrong and is now 'write-off when a clawback receivable proves unrecoverable' — the policy branch after chargeback_lost/farmer-paid, which the event now references. 1000/1050/1100/4030 are deliberately chartered ahead of their flows (bank payout, token rail, clearing-tender variant, breakage sweep) and the chart now says which demo events exercise what.

challenges.q: The spec preaches effective_at + posted_at from day one, but the demo events carry no timestamps, and global farmer_paid/dispute_open flags conflate concurrent orders.
challenges.a: Conceded as a demo limitation, now practiced where cheap: every journal entry displays its effective day and posted sequence (the two clocks, visibly divergent on dispute outcomes), and the page states its scope — one canonical order per scenario, reset to restart. The row schema in §6 remains the spec.

challenges.q: The 5010/5000 reclass is a net-zero shuffle between two expense accounts presented as refund economics, and prorating it implied a fractional fee loss when Stripe keeps the whole original fee.
challenges.a: Conceded. The reclass legs are removed from the refund events — the fee is fully expensed at capture (per C-2) and simply stays sunk; the narratives now say so. 5010 remains chartered as an OPTIONAL net-zero disclosure reclass for attributing sunk fees to refund losses, clearly labeled as such.

challenges.q: 4000 reads as a 10% take while the contractor 3% comes out of it — net ~7% is never shown next to it.
challenges.a: Conceded. The capture take memo and the contractor_accrual narrative now cross-reference each other and state net take = 4000 − 5200 while a drop is in-window, matching take_rate_params.
